reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generates the audio following the user's provided script with high accuracy. There is a very minor content discrepancy in the transcript where 'there are people' is rendered as 'There people' (omitting 'are', or eliding it heavily) and 'Dawdling' is transcribed as 'doddling', but the core meaning remains intact. The pacing indicated by the timestamps (short bursts of 2-3 seconds per phrase) suggests a natural flow consistent with an impatient or rude attitude, effectively delivering the urgency and annoyance required by the prompt.
reasoning_model_2: The model completely failed to follow the primary constraint of the prompt, which was to 'read this sentence'. Instead of synthesizing the target text ('Hey, are you going or not?...'), the model generated a completely different, improvised monologue ('So you\'re just standing there dawdling like you own the place?...'). According to the evaluation criteria, a score of 0 is assigned when the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, regardless of the emotional tone achieved in the hallucinated text.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) followed the text instructions and generated the correct script with the intended attitude, showing only negligible transcription/pronunciation deviations. Model 2 (T2) ignored the text-to-speech constraint entirely and hallucinated new content. While T2's generated text was indeed rude, the complete failure in Content Accuracy makes it a catastrophic failure according to the scoring standards. Therefore, Model 1 wins decisively.
